design_guidelines.md.content:
# StudyBot Design Guidelines

## Design Approach

**Selected System:** Material Design 3 with educational app influences (inspired by Quizlet, Notion, and Khan Academy)

**Justification:** StudyBot is a utility-focused educational tool where clarity, efficiency, and ease of learning are paramount. Material Design provides excellent patterns for information-dense interfaces while maintaining visual appeal for student engagement.

**Core Principles:**
- Clarity over decoration: Every element serves a functional purpose
- Progressive disclosure: Show information as needed, avoid overwhelming users
- Accessibility-first: High contrast, clear hierarchy, keyboard navigation
- Mobile-responsive: Students study on various devices

## Typography

**Font Family:** 
- Primary: 'Inter' (Google Fonts) - clean, highly legible for UI and body text
- Code/Output: 'JetBrains Mono' - for displaying AI responses and technical content

**Hierarchy:**
- H1 (App Title): text-4xl (36px), font-bold, leading-tight
- H2 (Section Headers): text-2xl (24px), font-semibold, leading-snug
- H3 (Feature Labels): text-lg (18px), font-medium
- Body Text: text-base (16px), font-normal, leading-relaxed
- Small Text (metadata): text-sm (14px), font-normal
- Button Text: text-base (16px), font-medium, tracking-wide

## Layout System

**Spacing Primitives:** Consistently use Tailwind units of 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, and 16
- Tight spacing: gap-2, p-2 (for compact elements)
- Standard spacing: gap-4, p-4, m-4 (most common use)
- Section spacing: py-8, py-12, py-16 (vertical rhythm)
- Container padding: px-4 on mobile, px-8 on desktop

**Container Structure:**
- Max width: max-w-4xl for main content (optimal reading width)
- Centered: mx-auto for horizontal centering
- Full viewport height NOT enforced - let content breathe naturally
- Responsive breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px)

**Grid System:**
- Single column on mobile (base)
- Two columns for feature cards on tablet/desktop (md:grid-cols-2)
- Use CSS Grid for card layouts, Flexbox for navigation and button groups

## Component Library

### Navigation Header
- Sticky top navigation (sticky top-0 z-50)
- Contains: Logo/title on left, minimal navigation links on right
- Height: h-16 on mobile, h-20 on desktop
- Add subtle shadow for depth: shadow-sm

### Hero Section
- Height: Natural height based on content (py-16 to py-24), NOT full viewport
- Layout: Two-column on desktop (md:grid-cols-2), stacked on mobile
- Left: Headline, subheadline, primary CTA button
- Right: Feature image or illustration showing students using the app
- Include social proof: Small badge like "Trusted by 10,000+ students"

### Main Interaction Panel
- Central card with rounded-xl borders
- Contains: Input field, action buttons in a horizontal row
- Input field: Large (h-12 to h-14), prominent placeholder text
- Buttons: Icon + text labels, min-w-[120px], arranged with gap-4
- Voice button: Distinctive with microphone icon
- Elevation: shadow-md for depth

### Output Display Area
- Card-style container with rounded-lg borders
- Minimum height: min-h-[300px] to prevent layout shift
- Typography: text-base with line-height-loose for readability
- For code/AI output: Use monospace font, syntax highlighting consideration
- Loading state: Subtle pulse animation while fetching

### Feature Cards (3 features: Tutor, Quiz, Plan)
- Grid layout: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 with gap-6
- Each card: p-6, rounded-lg, with icon at top
- Icon: Large size (h-12 w-12), centered above title
- Structure: Icon → Title (font-semibold) → Description (text-sm)
- Hover state: subtle lift effect (hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow)

### Action Buttons
- Primary: Large (px-6 py-3), rounded-lg, font-medium
- Secondary: Outlined style with border-2, same padding
- Icon buttons: Square (h-12 w-12), rounded-full for voice input
- Button group spacing: gap-3 between buttons
- All buttons: transition-all duration-200 for smooth interactions

### Footer
- Simple, informative footer with py-8
- Contains: Quick links, GitHub/source link, brief description
- Layout: Centered on mobile, multi-column on desktop
- Include: "Made for students" tagline or similar

## Images

**Hero Image:** 
Include a modern, friendly illustration or photo showing:
- Students collaborating or studying with technology
- Clean, contemporary aesthetic (avoid stock photo look)
- Placement: Right side of hero section on desktop, above content on mobile
- Dimensions: Approximately 600x400px, optimized for web
- Style: Match the educational, approachable tone - consider illustrations over photos

**Feature Icons:**
Use Heroicons (via CDN) for consistency:
- Tutor feature: academic-cap icon
- Quiz feature: clipboard-document-check icon  
- Study Plan feature: calendar-days icon
- Voice input: microphone icon

## Accessibility Standards

- All interactive elements minimum 44x44px touch target
- Form inputs with clear labels (sr-only for visual but accessible labels)
- Focus states: ring-2 ring-offset-2 for keyboard navigation
- ARIA labels for icon-only buttons
- Semantic HTML: proper heading hierarchy, <main>, <nav>, <footer>
- High contrast maintained throughout (checked against WCAG AA)

## Responsive Behavior

**Mobile (base - sm):**
- Single column layout throughout
- Stacked buttons (flex-col gap-3)
- Input field full width
- Reduced padding (p-4 instead of p-8)

**Tablet (md):**
- Two-column hero section
- Horizontal button row
- Feature cards in 2-column grid

**Desktop (lg):**
- Three-column feature grid
- Maximum container width enforced (max-w-4xl)
- Generous whitespace and padding

## Animations

**Minimal, Purposeful Only:**
- Loading states: Gentle pulse on output area while AI processes
- Button interactions: Standard hover/active states (no custom animations)
- Page transitions: None - instant rendering for snappy feel
- Voice input: Optional subtle pulse on microphone button when active

## Special Considerations

**Voice Interaction Indicators:**
- Clear visual feedback when voice recognition is active
- Microphone button changes state (add "listening" indicator)
- Transcribed text appears in input field with brief highlight

**AI Response Formatting:**
- Preserve markdown-like formatting from AI (bullet points, numbering)
- Quiz questions: Format as numbered list with clear answer options
- Study plans: Use structured day-by-day layout, possibly accordion pattern

**Error States:**
- Toast notifications for API errors (top-right, auto-dismiss)
- Inline validation for empty input submissions
- Friendly error messages ("Oops! Let's try that again")

client/src/components/ui/progress.tsx.content:
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import * as ProgressPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-progress"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Progress = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, value, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ProgressPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative h-4 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ProgressPrimitive.Indicator
      className="h-full w-full flex-1 bg-primary transition-all"
      style={{ transform: `translateX(-${100 - (value || 0)}%)` }}
    />
  </ProgressPrimitive.Root>
))
Progress.displayName = ProgressPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Progress }

replit.md.content:
# StudyBot - AI College Assistant

## Overview

StudyBot is an educational web application that provides AI-powered learning assistance for college students. The application offers three core features: an AI tutor that explains topics in simple terms, an interactive quiz generator that creates practice questions with answer checking and scoring, and a study plan creator that organizes learning schedules. The application uses Google Gemini API to generate educational content, includes text-to-speech capabilities for audio explanations, and persists user data including quiz results to a PostgreSQL database for progress tracking.

## User Preferences

Preferred communication style: Simple, everyday language.

## System Architecture

### Frontend Architecture

**Framework**: React with TypeScript using Vite as the build tool

**UI Component System**: Shadcn/ui components built on Radix UI primitives
- Material Design 3 principles with educational app influences
- Component library includes 40+ pre-built UI components (buttons, cards, forms, dialogs, etc.)
- Custom theming system using CSS variables for consistent design tokens
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach using Tailwind CSS breakpoints

**State Management**: TanStack Query (React Query) for server state management
- Centralized query client configuration
- Custom API request wrapper with error handling
- Mutations for POST requests to AI endpoints
- No global client-side state - relies on server data and local component state

**Routing**: Wouter for lightweight client-side routing
- Single-page application with route-based code splitting
- Routes: Home, Quiz History, Landing (unauthenticated), 404 fallback
- Protected routes require authentication via Replit Auth

**Design System**:
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- Custom color palette with semantic color tokens (primary, secondary, muted, destructive, etc.)
- Typography using Inter font for UI and JetBrains Mono for code
- Spacing system based on consistent units (2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16)
- Max content width of 4xl for optimal reading experience

### Backend Architecture

**Runtime**: Node.js with Express.js web framework

**Language**: TypeScript with ES modules

**API Design**: RESTful endpoints with JSON request/response format
- `/api/tutor` - POST endpoint for topic explanations
- `/api/quiz` - POST endpoint for quiz generation (3 multiple-choice questions)
- `/api/quiz/save` - POST endpoint for saving quiz results (protected)
- `/api/quiz/history` - GET endpoint for retrieving user quiz history (protected)
- `/api/plan` - POST endpoint for study plan creation

**Request Validation**: Zod schemas for type-safe request validation
- Shared schema definitions between client and server
- Runtime type checking and validation
- Automatic TypeScript type inference from schemas

**Development Setup**:
- Vite middleware integration for hot module replacement in development
- Custom logging middleware for API request tracking
- Static file serving for production builds

### Data Storage

**Database**: PostgreSQL using Drizzle ORM
- Neon serverless Postgres driver for connection pooling
- Database migrations stored in `/migrations` directory
- Schema definitions in `shared/schema.ts`
- Database storage implementation active with tables for:
  - `users` - User profiles (id, email, firstName, profileImageUrl)
  - `sessions` - PostgreSQL-backed session storage
  - `quizResults` - Quiz attempt history with scores
  - `savedStudyPlans` - User study plans
  - `flashcardDecks` - Flashcard collections (schema defined, feature pending)

**Session Management**: connect-pg-simple for PostgreSQL-backed sessions
- Cookie-based session storage with 7-day expiry
- Secure session management with automatic cleanup
- User authentication fully implemented via Replit Auth

### External Dependencies

**AI Service**: Google Gemini API (gemini-2.5-flash and gemini-2.5-pro models)
- API key required via `GEMINI_API_KEY` environment variable
- Using @google/genai SDK (GA version, official 2025 release)
- Models: gemini-2.5-flash for explanations, gemini-2.5-pro for structured JSON generation
- Structured prompts with JSON schema validation for quiz questions and study plans
- Implementation follows official 2025 documentation patterns

**Database Service**: Neon Serverless PostgreSQL
- Connection via `DATABASE_URL` environment variable
- Serverless architecture for automatic scaling
- WebSocket-based connection protocol

**Third-Party UI Libraries**:
- Radix UI primitives for accessible component foundations
- Embla Carousel for carousel functionality
- React Hook Form with Zod resolvers for form management
- Lucide React for icon components

**Development Tools**:
- Replit-specific plugins for development environment integration
- Cartographer plugin for code mapping
- Runtime error overlay for debugging

**Browser APIs**:
- Web Speech API for text-to-speech functionality
- Speech Recognition API for voice input (planned feature)

### Authentication & Authorization

**Replit Auth Integration**: OAuth-based authentication using Replit's OIDC provider
- Login/logout endpoints: `/api/login` and `/api/logout`
- User information endpoint: `/api/auth/user` 
- Session validation middleware (`isAuthenticated`) protects user data routes
- User profile data includes: email, first name, profile image URL
- Automatic user creation on first login
- Landing page for unauthenticated users with app overview
- Protected routes redirect to home after login

### Design Patterns

**Separation of Concerns**: Clear separation between client, server, and shared code
- `/client` - React frontend application
- `/server` - Express backend API
- `/shared` - Shared TypeScript types and Zod schemas

**Type Safety**: End-to-end TypeScript with shared schema definitions
- Zod schemas as single source of truth for data structures
- Type inference from schemas to TypeScript interfaces
- Runtime validation aligned with compile-time types

**Component Composition**: Radix UI slot pattern for flexible component APIs
- Components accept `asChild` prop for render delegation
- Consistent prop interfaces across similar components
- Accessible by default with ARIA attributes

**Error Handling**: Centralized error handling with user-friendly messages
- Try-catch blocks around all AI API calls
- Zod validation errors mapped to 400 responses
- Toast notifications for user-facing errors

**Progressive Enhancement**: Core functionality works without JavaScript
- Server-side rendering preparation with Vite SSR support
- Semantic HTML structure
- Accessibility-first component design

## Recent Changes

### Emotion-Aware AI Feature (Completed - November 8, 2025)

**User Experience**:
- AI automatically detects user mood from topic input (motivated, tired, bored, confused, stressed, confident)
- Mood indicator card displays current emotional state with emoji and icon
- Manual mood override dropdown for user control
- AI tutor adapts explanation tone and difficulty based on detected mood:
  - **Motivated**: Upbeat tone, challenging concepts, detailed examples
  - **Tired**: Simple language, short sentences, concise explanations
  - **Bored**: Engaging examples, fun facts, real-world applications
  - **Confused**: Step-by-step breakdowns, analogies, extra clarity
  - **Stressed**: Calm tone, bite-sized chunks, core concepts
  - **Confident**: Advanced concepts, nuanced details, deeper insights
- Text-to-speech voice adjusts rate and pitch based on mood:
  - Motivated/Confident: 1.1x rate, higher pitch
  - Tired/Stressed: 0.9x rate, lower pitch
  - Other moods: ~1.0x rate with variations
- Mood history saved to localStorage (last 50 entries)

**Technical Implementation**:
- Backend endpoint POST `/api/emotion` using Gemini 2.5-flash with JSON schema
- Returns emotion type + confidence score (0-1)
- Tutor endpoint accepts optional `mood` parameter
- 6 distinct prompt adaptations for personalized learning
- Frontend: React state + localStorage for mood persistence
- Auto-detects mood on every explanation request (>10 chars)
- Manual override with Select dropdown component
- Privacy-focused: localStorage only, no server-side mood storage

### Study Plan Dashboard with Edit & Progress Tracking (Completed - November 8, 2025)

**User Experience**:
- Generate study plans (any number of days) on home page using AI
- Save plans to database with "Save Plan" button
- View all saved plans on dedicated Study Plans page with full dashboard functionality:
  - **Edit Mode**: Click Edit button to modify plan subject inline with Save/Cancel controls
  - **Progress Tracking**: Checkbox for each topic to mark as complete with strike-through styling
  - **Progress Visualization**: Percentage badge showing completion rate, progress bar appears when progress > 0
  - **Deletion**: Delete unwanted plans with confirmation dialog
- Per-plan loading states during async operations (edit, delete, progress updates)
- Empty state messaging for new users

**Technical Implementation**:
- Backend endpoints (all protected, verify user ownership):
  - POST `/api/plan` - Generate plan (returns subject/days/plan data)
  - POST `/api/plans/save` - Save plan to database
  - GET `/api/plans/saved` - Retrieve user's saved plans
  - PATCH `/api/plans/:id` - Edit plan subject (validates userId ownership)
  - POST `/api/plans/:id/progress` - Update completed topics (validates userId ownership)
  - DELETE `/api/plans/:id` - Delete specific plan (validates userId ownership)
- Frontend features:
  - Edit mode with inline subject editing, optimistic UI updates
  - Checkbox-based progress tracking with immediate visual feedback
  - Progress calculation: (completedTopics.length / totalTopics) * 100
  - TanStack Query mutations with cache invalidation on all changes
  - Runtime validation for plan.plan array structure
  - Per-plan state tracking (editingPlanId, deletingPlanId) prevents UI conflicts
- Database:
  - `savedStudyPlans` table (snake_case: `saved_study_plans`) with JSONB columns:
    - `plan`: Array of {day: string, topics: string[]} objects
    - `completedTopics`: Array of string identifiers ("day-0-topic-1" format)
  - Automatic JSONB parsing by PostgreSQL driver
- Security:
  - All mutation endpoints validate userId + id to prevent unauthorized access
  - Returns 404 when plan not found or not owned by user
  - NaN guards on plan ID parameters

**Key Implementation Details**:
- Progress tracking uses string identifiers: `day-${dayIndex}-topic-${topicIndex}`
- Strike-through styling applied to completed topics for visual feedback
- Progress badge shows 0% initially, updates in real-time as topics are checked
- Progress bar component only visible when progress > 0%
- Edit mode preserves plan structure, only allows subject modification
- Per-plan deletion tracking prevents blocking other plans during async operations

## Features

### Interactive Quiz System (Completed)

**User Experience**:
- Users generate quizzes on any topic using AI
- Three multiple-choice questions per quiz (A, B, C, D options)
- Click-to-select answer interface with visual feedback
- Submit button enabled only after all questions are answered
- Real-time scoring with color-coded results:
  - Correct answers: green highlight
  - Incorrect user selections: red highlight
  - Feedback messages for each question
- Score display with encouragement message
- Results automatically saved to database
- "Try Another Quiz" button to generate new quizzes

**Technical Implementation**:
- State management: `userAnswers` (Record<number, string>), `quizSubmitted` (boolean), `quizScore` (number)
- Answer selection tracked per question index
- Mutation to save results: POST `/api/quiz/save`
- React Query cache invalidation for real-time history updates
- Toast notifications for save confirmation/errors

### Quiz History Page (Completed)

**User Experience**:
- Accessible via navigation menu (authenticated users only)
- Displays all past quiz attempts with:
  - Quiz topic and date/time taken
  - Score (X / 3) with color-coded badge
  - Performance icon (Trophy for perfect, Award for >70%, Medal for >50%, Target for <50%)
  - Expandable question details showing user answers and correct answers
  - Visual indicators: ✓ for correct, ✗ for incorrect
- Empty state message for new users
- Badge showing total number of quizzes completed

**Technical Implementation**:
- Query endpoint: GET `/api/quiz/history`
- Uses TanStack Query for data fetching
- date-fns for date formatting
- Lucide React icons for visual feedback (no emojis)
- Responsive card layout with hover effects

client/src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as CheckboxPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-checkbox"
import { Check } from "lucide-react"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Checkbox = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <CheckboxPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "peer h-4 w-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-primary ring-offset-background focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=checked]:text-primary-foreground",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator
      className={cn("flex items-center justify-center text-current")}
    >
      <Check className="h-4 w-4" />
    </CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator>
  </CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
))
Checkbox.displayName = CheckboxPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Checkbox }

  </SliderPrimitive.Root>
))
Slider.displayName = SliderPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Slider }

client/src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as ScrollAreaPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const ScrollArea = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("relative overflow-hidden", className)}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport className="h-full w-full rounded-[inherit]">
      {children}
    </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport>
    <ScrollBar />
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Corner />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
))
ScrollArea.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root.displayName

const ScrollBar = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
>(({ className, orientation = "vertical", ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar
    ref={ref}
    orientation={orientation}
    className={cn(
      "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
      orientation === "vertical" &&
        "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
      orientation === "horizontal" &&
        "h-2.5 flex-col border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaThumb className="relative flex-1 rounded-full bg-border" />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
))
ScrollBar.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar.displayName

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar }
